title: RIAA recognises top responsible super funds amid ESG scrutiny
full_text:
The Responsible Investment Association Australasia (RIAA) has named 12 super funds as Australia’s 2026 Responsible Super Fund Leaders, recognising organisations it said are setting the benchmark for responsible investment as scrutiny of environmental, social and governance (ESG) practices intensifies.
Announced at the RIAA Conference Australia, the 2026 recognitions included 12 Responsible Super Fund Leaders, 47 Responsible Investment Leaders and 25 Responsible Investors.
According to RIAA, this year’s recognitions come as ESG faces heightened global debate, driving stronger expectations around governance, transparency and accountability across financial markets.
“Responsible investment is entering a more mature phase, where leadership is defined by what organisations do, not just what they say,” said Estelle Parker, co-chief executive of RIAA.
“We’re seeing leading organisations lean into transparency, governance and evidence, translating intent into practice through clearer disclosure, stronger reporting and the use of independent verification. That’s building confidence for investors and strengthening trust across the market.”
The 2026 Responsible Super Fund Leaders are:
- Australian Ethical Investment,
- Australian Retirement Trust,
- AustralianSuper,
- Aware Super,
- CareSuper,
- Cbus Super,
- Equip Super,
- HESTA,
- Hostplus,
- Rest,
- TelstraSuper
- UniSuper.
Responsible Super Fund Leaders are the highest-scoring super funds assessed across governance, commitment, implementation, outcomes and transparency, while Responsible Investment Leaders and Responsible Investors are assessed across commitment, ESG integration, stewardship and capital allocation.
RIAA said organisations recognised this year demonstrated strong governance frameworks, systematic integration of ESG factors into investment decision-making and transparent reporting on long-term outcomes and stewardship activities, including the use of independent verification.
It added these practices were helping lift standards across the market through more consistent disclosure, improved visibility of ESG risk management processes and clearer evidence of how responsible investment approaches are being implemented.
The association’s leadership recognitions assess organisations on a whole-of-organisation basis using surveys, public disclosures and structured evidence.
Commenting on the recognition, UniSuper said: “In recognising UniSuper as a Responsible Super Fund Leader, RIAA has acknowledged our commitment to responsible investment practices, our strong governance and focus on delivering measurable outcomes. The recognition reflects our whole-of-fund responsible investment approach as well as our dedication to transparency.
“As one of Australia’s largest super funds, and with over 70 per cent of our investments managed in-house, our team can engage with the boards and management of our largest Australian investments. This allows us to discuss a range of issues with these companies and feeds into our decision-making. As an active owner, we also seek to vote our shares at company meetings.”
author: Adrian Suljanovic
heading: Superannuation and Investments
